layers.material: porous PTFE, PTFE, diaphragm, separator, membrane

gas_params.cathode_flow: 10-50 sccm, feed from generated recommendation: CO
gas_params.anode_flow: 10-50 sccm, feed from generated recommendation: not specified
gas_params.spray: 1 M KOH aerosol, controlled droplet loading

test_plan: Build a two-electrode CO reduction flow reactor with identical geometry except for separator choice: porous PTFE diaphragm/separator versus incumbent ion-exchange membrane.
Use the same supported polycrystalline Cu powder on PTFE-treated carbon fiber paper cathode in both cells.
Feed gaseous CO with a controlled aerosol containing H2O and K+ to the cathode so that cation and water delivery are assigned to the gas-aerosol-solid path.
Record polarization, high-frequency resistance, product distribution/current efficiency, flooding onset, and stability over a fixed galvanostatic hold.
notes: A CO-reduction flow reactor using a gas-aerosol-solid contacting cathode, with a supported polycrystalline Cu powder gas diffusion electrode on PTFE-treated carbon fiber paper and a porous PTFE diaphragm/separator used only for physical phase isolation/cost reduction, while H2O and K+ are delivered through the cathode aerosol stream rather than through the separator.
